type: CLAIM
files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py

fields.note: I'll own both shared files and implement IsHash there plus your IsPhoneNumber per your SPEC. Please DEFER these files and send SPEC for IsPhoneNumber (class behavior, __init__ signature, validation logic, __all__ entry, alphabetical placement).

content:
type: DEFER
files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
spec: Add class IsHash(DirtyEquals[str]) to dirty_equals/_other.py, placed after the IsUUID class. Signature: def __init__(self, algorithm: str) -> None. Supported algorithms (case-insensitive lookup, but store/display original case as passed by user): 'md5' (32 hex chars), 'sha1' (40 hex chars), 'sha224' (56 hex chars), 'sha256' (64 hex chars), 'sha384' (96 hex chars), 'sha512' (128 hex chars), 'sha3_224' (56), 'sha3_256' (64), 'sha3_384' (96), 'sha3_512' (128). Use a class-level dict mapping lowercased algorithm name -> expected hex length. In __init__, validate algorithm.lower() is a known key (raise ValueError with message like f'Unknown hash algorithm {algorithm!r}' if not), store self.algorithm = algorithm (preserve original case) and self.length = <expected length>. Implement def equals(self, other: Any) -> bool: return isinstance(other, str) and len(other) == self.length and all(c in string.hexdigits for c in other) (use re.fullmatch(rf'[0-9a-fA-F]{{{self.length}}}', other) instead of manual loop is fine too). Implement def __repr__ / use the DirtyEquals repr mechanism so that repr looks like IsHash('md5') -- i.e. pass algorithm through self._repr_args or equivalent existing pattern used by other classes in _other.py (check IsUUID/IsJson for the pattern already in file, e.g. they likely override __repr__ or rely on DirtyEquals default using __init__ args recorded via a decorator/self._other_args). Just make str(IsHash('md5')) == "IsHash('md5')". Then in dirty_equals/__init__.py: add IsHash to the 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID' line (alphabetical: FunctionCheck, IsHash, IsJson, IsUUID) and add 'IsHash,' to the __all__ tuple in the same group as IsJson/IsUUID (alphabetical order there too). No changes needed to any other file for my feature.
note: IsHash validator spec
